name: ADRENOCORTICAL ADENOMA

demographics.Prevalence: 1-2% in general population; age-dependent 6.6-8.7% at autopsies (small tumors in
demographics.Path: atypical intralesional cystic degeneration / less often hemorrhage
demographics.Histo: clear cells arranged in cords with abundant intracellular (= intracytoplasmic) pale-

dominant_finding: < 37 HU on 5-15-min delayed CECT is DIAGNOSTIC of adenoma
distribution: unilateral focus of 131I NP-59 radioactivity + contralateral absence of iodocholesterol
discriminating_features: < 37 HU on 5-15-min delayed CECT is DIAGNOSTIC of adenoma

general: small adenomas < 1 cm often go undetected
NECT: soft-tissue density / cystic density (mimicked by high cholesterol content) with poor
CECT: < 37 HU on 5-15-min delayed CECT is DIAGNOSTIC of adenoma
ANGIO: enlarged central vein with high flow
bilateral adrenal venous sampling → in up to 40% unsuccessful in localizing
MR: isointense mass relative to liver + hyperintense relative to spleen on T1WI ← short T1
iso- / hypointense mass (rarely hyperintense) to spleen on T2WI
marked hypointensity compared with spleen / skeletal muscle on opposed-phase GRE
signal loss of ≥ 20% on opposed-phase image
India ink effect = characteristic black lines outlining interface between organ + adjacent fat
small round foci of altered SI ← cystic changes / hemorrhage / variation in vascularity
uniform enhancement on immediate early imaging
adenomas tend to enhance less rapidly + less intensely than metastases on time-
relatively rapid washout of contrast material compared with metastases with return to
US: well-circumscribed round or oval lesion of slightly heterogeneous mixed echogenicity
adenoma may calcify → often in areas of old hemorrhage
surveillance CT to confirm lack of growth
contralateral adrenal atrophy ← suppression of pituitary ACTH by elevated cortisol levels
ACTH infusion incites a dramatic increase in levels of cortisol + aldosterone for
contralateral atrophic gland ← ACTH suppression with autonomous adenoma
unilateral focus of 131I NP-59 radioactivity + contralateral absence of iodocholesterol

clinical.Rx: surgical removal for masses 3-5 cm as indeterminate potentially malignant neoplasms
